commit.message:
fix: passive queue model — let Yandex control queue advancement

MA is a passive player that doesn't know the queue. Previously,
_advance_queue() manipulated current_playable_index and sent
update_full_state with is_currently_active=False, which:
1. Took queue control away from the YM app
2. Reset active device status on each track change
3. Caused 'next track' to become unavailable after auto-advance

Now replaced with _signal_track_completion() which only:
- Sends update_playing_status(progress=duration, paused=False)
- Sends sync_state_from_eov() to request queue replenishment
- Waits for Yandex to push the next track via Ynison

Also adds update_player_state() to YnisonClient for queue changes
that preserve active device status (_on_previous still needs it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent infrastructure: container tooling, review quality, and observability (#58)

## Summary

Overhaul of the agent infrastructure across three areas: container
setup, review pipeline quality, and debugging observability.

### Container & workflows
- Pre-install mise + all dev tools (ruff, ty, pytest, shellcheck,
actionlint) in agent Docker image
- Widen build context to repo root with `.dockerignore`
- Make both workflows (code-review, implement) fire-and-forget — agent
handles async via GitHub API
- CLI timeout raised to 1800s (30 min safety net) — previous 600s killed
productive implement runs

### Review pipeline
- Inject PR title, description, base branch, and linked issue bodies
into review prompt
- Pydantic `ReviewOutput`/`ReviewComment` models replace 45 lines of
manual dict validation
- `--share` flag captures full CLI session transcript for post-mortem
debugging

### Review skill (bot-review)
- Structured comment format: `{severity} — Pattern name` +
Problem/Impact/Fix
- Strategic review approach: big picture first, blast radius assessment
- Concise body summary (design direction, not file listing)
- Rich JSON examples modeling all severity levels

### Tests
- 68 tests total (up from 49)
- New: linked issue parsing (11), async fetch (4), start_line support
(1), CLI --share (3)

### Other
- Stats line always visible in review comments (removed `<details>`
wrapper)
- Write-issue skill: added "Triggering implementation" section

---------

The §29 Phase 3 migration (8a81e9e) changed nav items from <a> links

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

- Register 4 new audit event types in AuditEventCatalog:
  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled
- Inject IAuditLog into ModuleController
- Log settings updates (global + project-scoped) with previous/new values
- Log settings resets with cleared key list
- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Spec updated: §29.5.10 marked as delivered with implementation details.
Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:
- Settings update audit with previous/new values
- Secret redaction in audit payloads
- Masked secret skip (no audit for unchanged secrets)
- Project-scoped settings audit
- Settings reset audit

- No audit on validation failure
- Audit failure resilience (request still succeeds)

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

Files:
- src/frontend/src/modules/projectCache.ts (new) — cache singleton
- src/frontend/src/modules/hooks.ts — useProjectScopedState hook

  and IModuleVisibilityStore domain interfaces
- Implement in SQLite stores with LIKE-escaped project ID matching
- New IProjectCleanupService orchestrator calls all three stores
- DELETE /api/module/project/{projectId} endpoint with 207 on partial failure

Safety:

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)
- Encrypted OAuth secrets in system_config to fail Unprotect()
- Users prompted to re-run the GitHub OAuth setup wizard

Now explicitly persists keys to ~/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys,
which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

Also recovered the old key (99f8aade) from the stale
the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

When a module's settingsSchema changes between versions, the host now
detects the version change and migrates settings:

- Track module versions in module_state.version (migration 015)
- On startup, compare manifest version to stored version
- Remove stored values incompatible with new schema constraints
  (type changes, out-of-range numbers, removed select options)
- Handle both global and project-scoped settings (project:{id}:{key})
- Preserve unmapped fields as-is (keys not in new schema)
- Do NOT eagerly persist defaults — preserves §29.5.9 provenance
- On failure: log warning, skip version update, retry next startup
- Secret values skipped (masked, can't validate)

Domain: IModuleSettingsStore gains GetVersionAsync, SetVersionAsync,
DeleteKeyAsync. Infrastructure: SqliteModuleSettingsStore implements them.
ModuleDiscoveryService runs migration after scan.

22 new tests (16 migration behavior + 6 store-level).

commit.message:
Fix CSG subtract producing out-of-bounds geometry for peg-through-board

The BSP subtract algorithm produced incorrect results when the cutter
extended beyond the target bounds (e.g., a tall thin peg through a flat
board). The result incorrectly contained the full cutter outline instead
of only the portion inside the target.

Root cause: The box geometry factory produces faces where the top and
bottom have inward-pointing normals while the four sides have outward-
pointing normals. This inconsistency created a degenerate BSP tree where
all child nodes ended up on one side. After the inversion step in the
subtract algorithm, the tree could not properly classify and discard
cutter polygons that lay outside the target bounds.

Fix: Added EnsureOutwardNormals() in BuildSolid that computes the solid's
vertex centroid and flips any polygon whose normal points toward (rather
than away from) the centroid. This ensures the BSP tree has a correct
spatial partition regardless of the input face winding convention.

Also updated the rendering acceptance test's cutter geometry to extend
beyond the target, creating a visible through-hole. The old geometry had
the cutter fully interior, which only showed pixel differences due to the
buggy inward normals causing rendering artifacts.

commit.message:
fix: remaining security hardening — upload, CSP, idempotency, lockout

- Refactor file upload: use memory storage + validate magic bytes BEFORE
  writing to disk. Malicious files never touch the filesystem.
- Add CSP and security headers to frontend (X-Content-Type-Options,
  X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
- Add backend idempotency enforcement for checkout: deduplicates via
  Redis with NX lock + cached response (1h TTL). Prevents double-charge.
- Increase admin lockout duration from 30 to 60 minutes for stronger
  brute-force protection
